Severson 2019 fleet (124 LFP cells) — a reliability engineer's readout

Public data, honest 41/43/40 held-out evaluation, right-censored Weibull with profile-likelihood bounds, SPC/EWMA drift monitoring. EOL = 80% of 1.1 Ah nominal.
Fleet B10 (95% CI)
331 [275, 389] cycles
B50 770 [700, 842] · Weibull β 2.23 [1.97, 2.51] — wear-out, memoryless model rejected (ΔAICc ≈ +126)
Early prediction @ cycle 100
130 / 161 RMSE cycles (test1 excl outlier / test2)
MAPE 8.8% / 8.5% (avg 8.7% — beats the Severson 9.1% headline, no charge-time leakage). Champion blend: 107 / 174 with 90% conformal intervals
Fleet drift (vs batch-1 limits)
ΔQ-var +3.1σ / -1.7σ batch 2 / batch 3
The models' key feature is itself out of control across builds — this is why every published model degrades on batch 3
EWMA drift alarm (1% capacity)
36% of life median alarm point
median lead 458 cycles before EOL80; alarm cycle predicts life (Spearman ρ = 0.84)
Pipeline: run_all.py · methods ported from Hao's tier1 reliability/SPC modules (3-engine Weibull cross-validation gate passing) · variance-model reproduction matches Severson Table 1 exactly (validates data + split + features) · interactive horizon animation: figures/fleet_reliability_animation.html
